column1: PdfReaderActivity::loadPage overflows the ActivityManager stack because it allocates a full PdfPage scratch object on the stack for cache warm-up
Moving the PdfPage scratch object off the stack eliminates the startup crash
PdfCachedPageReader::close() must tolerate failed or missing opens because HalStorage still leaves a HalFile wrapper object after open failures
Guarding PdfCachedPageReader::close() with file_.isOpen() prevents the HalFile::close assert after failed cache-page opens
The direct host pdf_parser_host_test binary was stale, so earlier preview dumps did not reflect the current parser implementation
The committed baseline ContentStream.cpp parser path passes the full host PDF parser suite, including esp32-c6_datasheet_en.pdf
The local experimental ContentStream changes caused the esp32-c6 spacing regressions; the cache and stack fixes were not the source of that parser breakage
Increasing PDF_MAX_TMP_RUNS only masked the experimental parser regression and is not needed on the passing parser baseline
Deferring PDF cache metadata writes until after the first successful page parse reduces startup-path SD work while preserving cache behavior
Removing redundant existence checks before SD open calls reduces repeated SdFat path work and keeps the startup path simpler
